# LearnScope
> Evidence-based management consulting for K-12 school districts

LearnScope designs research-driven tools and services to help district leaders build organizational capacity and deliver results for their communities. Founded by Adam Hogue, former SVP at BetterLesson, with degrees from Harvard College and Yale School of Management.

## Core Framework: School System Capacity Model

LearnScope's School System Capacity Model identifies three interdependent dimensions that determine whether a district can reliably translate investments into outcomes:

- Clarity: The foundation for simplification, alignment, and focus. Districts with strong capacity have an unusually clear picture of what they are trying to accomplish, who is responsible for what, and how they will know if their efforts are working.
- Coherence: The discipline of making clarity actionable. Programs, staffing, professional development, and resource allocation working in concert rather than in competition.
- Collective Learning: The infrastructure that sustains capacity over time. Systems for monitoring implementation quality, feedback loops connecting classrooms to district decisions, and routines that help educators learn from one another.

## Organizational Network Analysis (ONA) for K–12 system leaders
- [How revealing the "hidden" organization amplifies the impact of K–12 system leaders](https://learnscope.co/blog/organizational-network-analysis-k12-system-leaders): Explains why formal authority alone cannot drive lasting change in school districts, and how organizational network analysis (ONA) reveals the informal networks of trust, expertise, and influence that determine whether reforms take hold. Includes a turnaround case study and a definition of ONA in the K–12 context.

## Key Concepts

- Initiative Debt: The accumulated organizational cost of adding programs, mandates, and initiatives without retiring, integrating, or adequately resourcing them. Similar to technical debt in software development.
- The Perfect Program Myth: The belief that selecting the right evidence-based program is sufficient to produce outcomes, without attending to the organizational capacity required to implement it well.
- The Autonomy Trap: When well-intentioned school-level autonomy produces system-wide fragmentation, making it harder for districts to learn, align, and improve.

## Services

LearnScope offers four persona-specific diagnostic engagements, all built on the
School System Capacity Model (three lenses: Clarity, Coherence, Collective
Learning) and organizational network analysis (ONA).

- [OrgScope](https://www.learnscope.co/orgscope): An evidence-based district
  diagnostic for incoming and interim superintendents. Maps the formal and
  hidden organization and grounds a first-180-days entry plan in data rather
  than listening tours alone.

- [ChangeScope](https://www.learnscope.co/changescope): An implementation-
  readiness diagnostic for chief academic officers launching high-stakes
  initiatives such as curriculum adoptions. Aligns vision, systems, and
  educator networks so new programs translate into classroom practice.

- [TalentScope](https://www.learnscope.co/talentscope): A talent-strategy
  diagnostic for chief talent officers. Assesses the educator talent pipeline,
  surfaces burnout and retention risk through network analysis, and supports a
  proactive talent strategy.

- [VendorScope](https://www.learnscope.co/vendorscope): A vendor and edtech
  portfolio diagnostic for chief operating officers. Audits tools, contracts,
  and usage data to reduce vendor sprawl, find consolidation opportunities,
  and reinvest in what works.
